Fourier mixing reference for cheap dense transforms.

Rotation plus product quantization as a direct compression analogue.

Incoherence processing and trellis quantization for low-bit LLM compression.

Kalman-style latent-state estimation and confidence-weighted measurement fusion.
Confidence-based adaptive routing and compute allocation.
Shrinkage-style blending when translated KV is noisy.

Head-weighting and dynamic head selection instead of flat concatenation.

Saliency-guided KV compression and token selection.

Outlier-aware adaptive rotations and grouped-head quantization.
Asymmetric key/value quantization baseline for the fused KV path.

Event-triggered communication based on confidence thresholds.

Residual-error transmission and iterative correction as a KV-comms analogue.
Model stitching evidence that transferable linear features exist across language models.
Theory grounding for when linear or near-linear cross-model alignment should succeed.
Spectral compression baseline for testing whether transformed latent signals are easier to transmit.
Exploratory multiresolution-attention reference for future frequency-aware head selection and routing.

Retrieval-head view of long-context inference; motivates selective K-only head retention.
Head-level KV cache retention directly supports the grouped-head and K-only branches.

KV-centric benchmark framing for evaluating communication and compression claims more directly.
Task-aware adaptive KV budgets support content-dependent communication schedules.
Reasoning-specific redundancy analysis for KV compression; especially relevant for GSM8K-style tasks.
Direct support for asymmetric treatment of keys and values, matching the emerging K-only result.
Evidence that keys and values behave differently across local and global cache structure, supporting the K-only branch.
Attention-fidelity objective for preserving QK structure rather than only raw KV reconstruction.
Periodic lossless retention suggests structured position-level sparsity instead of uniform transmission.
Direct token-level KV selection method for the next sparse-key transport ablations.
Primary query-aware sparsity reference for selective key-position retention instead of uniform transport.
Reliability-weighted KV fusion baseline that can inform source-dependent blending instead of one fixed gate.
Supports attention-space preservation objectives when KV reconstruction alone is not predictive of downstream quality.
Query-centric cache fusion is a direct architectural reference for source-conditioned fusion beyond scalar gate sweeps.
